name: Governance Classifier
one_liner: Automatically routes incoming board submissions, audit findings, and policy notices to the correct oversight body — diocesan, government regulator, accreditation board, or funder committee — based on document type and escalation threshold.
problem: In multi-stakeholder school networks like PHINMA and CSPD, governance documents arrive from a mix of sources — Catholic diocesan offices, CHED, NESA, accreditation panels, investor committees — each requiring a different approval pathway. Board secretaries manually decide where each document goes, creating routing delays and compliance gaps when documents sit in the wrong queue.
how_it_works: Ingests documents submitted to the governance inbox (board papers, audit reports, policy change notices, accreditation correspondence). Applies a multi-label classifier to identify regulatory domain (Catholic governance, government compliance, accreditation evidence, financial reporting) and urgency. Outputs a routing decision with confidence score, assigns the document to the correct committee queue, and flags anomalies — e.g., a document with accreditation implications that was submitted only to the finance committee.
ai_technique: Text classification with multi-label hierarchical routing
buyer_persona: Governance Officer / Compliance Manager / Board Secretary
scoring_rationale: Document routing is a real pain in complex multi-regulator organisations, but the problem is solved intermittently rather than daily — routing failures are annoying, not catastrophic. The concept is nearly identical to Governance Document Classifier (idea 77) in this same batch, limiting standalone viability.

name: Governance Anomaly Detector
one_liner: Monitors board decision timelines, audit finding resolution rates, and procurement approval patterns to surface governance integrity risks before they become reportable incidents.
problem: At PHINMA (publicly listed, ASX-equivalent obligations) and WISE (Qatar Foundation accountability), governance processes can drift silently — board decisions delayed without explanation, audit findings that stall for months, procurement approvals that bunch in unusual patterns. By the time these anomalies surface in an external audit, the reputational and regulatory cost is already significant. Current board management software has no education-specific anomaly layer.
how_it_works: Connects to board management system event logs, committee meeting records, audit resolution trackers, and procurement approval histories. Runs statistical baselines for each process type — e.g., median approval cycle for policy changes, expected resolution time per audit finding category. Flags deviations: a governance committee that has not met on schedule, an audit finding in 'under review' status for twice the normal period, a procurement approver whose decision volume spikes in the weeks before year-end. Delivers a weekly governance health digest with ranked anomalies and supporting evidence.
ai_technique: Anomaly detection on board management system event logs and timelines
buyer_persona: Audit Committee Chair / Chief Governance Officer
scoring_rationale: Genuine governance risk intelligence with a clear buyer (Audit Committee Chair), but data access is the hard constraint — structured event logs from board management systems are inconsistently available and often require custom integration per organisation. Functionally overlaps with idea 62 (Governance Anomaly Monitor) from the full list.

name: Governance Evidence Dashboard
one_liner: Maps governance documents, committee reports, and policy records to their specific regulatory requirements — CHED accreditation matrices, diocesan policy expectations, funder templates — showing compliance status and evidence gaps in a live dashboard.
problem: Education organisations subject to multiple concurrent regulatory regimes — CHED, diocesan oversight, international funders, ASX-equivalent obligations — have no unified view of their governance evidence. Leaders manually assemble evidence packages at each review cycle, without a running record of what is filed, what is missing, and what has expired. Cross-regulator gaps — where the same process must satisfy two standards — are invisible until a reviewer flags them.
how_it_works: Ingests regulatory requirement frameworks from CHED accreditation matrices, AITSL standards, diocesan policy handbooks, and funder reporting templates. Using RAG over the organisation's governance document repository, maps existing evidence (board papers, committee reports, policy versions, meeting minutes) to each requirement. Displays a compliance matrix: green (evidence filed, current), amber (evidence present but dated), red (no evidence found). Surfacesdeadlines for upcoming review cycles and recommends document collection priorities.
ai_technique: Data transformation and structuring with education-specific taxonomy mapping
scoring_rationale: The multi-regulator evidence mapping problem is real and underserved, but this overlaps substantially with idea 61 (Accreditation Evidence Aggregator) and idea 76 (Accreditation Evidence Map) in this batch. Accreditation Lead is a clear buyer but has limited budget authority compared to a CFO or CEO.

name: Inquiry Intent Classifier
one_liner: Reads incoming SMS, WhatsApp, and email inquiries from prospective families and scores them by enrollment intent, program fit, and financial risk — routing warm leads to counsellors and flagging at-risk applicants before they go cold.
problem: In price-sensitive markets like West Africa and the Philippines, admissions teams receive large volumes of unstructured inquiries across multiple channels. Counsellors manually read each message to decide who to call back first — a slow process that means high-intent families wait while low-intent inquiries get equal attention. There is no education-specific signal parsing in standard CRMs to distinguish a family ready to enrol from one still shopping competitors.
how_it_works: Connects to inbound messaging channels (SMS gateway, WhatsApp Business API, email inbox). Classifies each message by intent type (enrolment inquiry, financial aid question, program information, complaint), program interest, financial-access signals (payment plan questions, scholarship mentions, sibling discounts), and urgency flags (deadline language, competitor mentions). Assigns a priority score and routes to the relevant counsellor queue with a one-line summary. Surfaces daily aggregate intent trends to the Admissions Manager.
ai_technique: Text classification on messaging data with education intent vocabulary
scoring_rationale: Clear problem, readily accessible data (messaging APIs), and fast time-to-value via counsellor queue improvements. However this is idea 17/39/72 under a different batch — a direct functional duplicate across the 81-idea list. As a standalone feature it lacks defensibility and is best positioned as a module within Enrolflow.

name: Enrollment Funnel Forecaster
one_liner: Forecasts next-semester cohort sizes by program using inquiry pipeline data, historical conversion rates, and academic calendar seasonality — giving admissions directors a 10-week lead on staffing and budget decisions.
problem: Admissions directors at multi-program institutions like iPeople and PHINMA make hiring and facility decisions based on last year's enrollment numbers, not a forward-looking view of the current pipeline. By the time actual enrollment becomes clear, it is too late to adjust instructor contracts, facility bookings, or scholarship budget. CRM systems only report what has happened, not what is likely to happen.
how_it_works: Ingests three data streams: historical enrollment records by program and semester, the current inquiry and application pipeline (volume, stage, age-of-inquiry), and the academic calendar (application deadlines, exam periods, scholarship announcement dates). Builds a per-program probabilistic forecast for the next two to three semesters, incorporating seasonal conversion curves fitted to institutional history. Outputs a forecast dashboard with confidence intervals, downside scenarios, and recommended recruitment intensity adjustments per program.
ai_technique: Time series forecasting with academic calendar features
scoring_rationale: Admissions Director is a real buyer with a budget pain point, and the forecasting logic is tractable with institutional data. However this is a direct duplicate of ideas 18 and 41 (Enrollment Funnel Forecaster, Cohort Conversion Funnel Forecaster) from the wider list. Belongs inside Enrolflow rather than as a standalone product.

name: Student Propensity Scorer
one_liner: Scores each applicant 0-100 on enrollment likelihood using prior school type, geographic access, family income proxies, and program fit — so outreach teams target scholarship and payment-plan offers at households most likely to convert.
problem: Institutions with large applicant volumes — PHINMA in affordable HE, Rising Academies targeting low-income households in West Africa — contact all applicants with the same outreach, regardless of conversion likelihood. High-propensity families miss targeted scholarship offers; low-propensity contacts consume counsellor time. Generic lead-scoring tools have no understanding of education-specific conversion signals.
how_it_works: Trains a classification model on three to five years of historical applicant records, using features available at application time: location relative to campus, type of secondary school attended, prior exam scores, program choice, parent education level (where collected), and payment plan election history for siblings. Produces a propensity score for each new applicant. Counsellor queues and outreach campaign lists are ranked by score. Score thresholds trigger automatic scholarship-offer or payment-plan prompts in the CRM.
ai_technique: Supervised classification on enrollment and demographic data
scoring_rationale: Education-specific propensity scoring has defensible domain logic once trained on institutional data, but this is a direct duplicate of ideas 7, 19, and 74 across the full list. Value is real but only as a module within Enrolflow, not a standalone product.

name: Board Meeting Transcript & Index
one_liner: Transcribes board and committee meeting recordings, tags governance entities (policy names, accreditation standards, financial decisions), and builds a searchable archive — so a board secretary can answer 'what did the board decide about staffing policy in 2023?' in seconds.
problem: Boards at PHINMA and School for Life must maintain auditable records of governance decisions, but transcription is done manually post-meeting and filing is inconsistent. When an accreditation body or regulator requests evidence of a specific decision, the board secretary spends hours searching PDF documents and email threads. Meeting recordings go unindexed and institutional governance memory is effectively locked inside audio files.
how_it_works: Accepts uploaded meeting recordings (video or audio) or live-session audio feed. Runs speech-to-text transcription and speaker diarisation. Applies a governance entity tagger to identify policy names, referenced accreditation standards, financial figures, decision outcomes, and named motion movers. Indexes the full transcript and entities in a searchable store. Exports a formatted minutes draft and an audit trail entry with decision date, motion text, and approver names. Governance officers can query the archive by topic, date range, or standard referenced.
ai_technique: Speech-to-text transcription with governance entity tagging
scoring_rationale: Meeting transcription + governance entity tagging is technically tractable with current APIs, time-to-value is immediate (first meeting produces a usable transcript), and the search-the-archive use case is genuinely painful for board secretaries. Lower defensibility as Whisper + RAG is replicable, but domain taxonomy creates some stickiness.

name: Accreditation Evidence Map
one_liner: Reads the organisation's governance documents, strategic plans, and operational reports and maps them to specific accreditation standard requirements — showing what evidence exists, what is missing, and what must be updated before the next review cycle.
problem: Preparing for a CHED or equivalent government accreditation review means manually cross-referencing dozens of standards against hundreds of institutional documents. The accreditation lead spends weeks pulling committee reports, policy versions, and board resolutions to fill evidence matrices — without confidence they have covered every requirement or that the evidence they found is sufficiently current. There is no live view of compliance status between review cycles.
how_it_works: Ingests the relevant accreditation standard framework (CHED matrix, NESA requirements, government oversight criteria) and the organisation's document repository (board papers, strategic plans, policy documents, committee reports). For each accreditation requirement, runs a RAG retrieval to find the strongest supporting evidence in the repository, assigns a coverage confidence score, and flags requirements with no match or only outdated documents. Outputs an evidence matrix showing standard, requirement text, mapped evidence, document date, and gap status. Sends alerts as review deadlines approach.
ai_technique: Document extraction and hierarchical data structuring against accreditation taxonomy
scoring_rationale: Accreditation prep is a high-severity, periodic pain point where teams genuinely struggle — RAG over institutional documents mapped to standard matrices is a strong agentic fit. Overlap with idea 61 (Accreditation Evidence Aggregator) and idea 71 (Governance Evidence Dashboard) in this batch limits standalone positioning.

name: Governance Document Classifier
one_liner: Categorises incoming board submissions, committee reports, and stakeholder correspondence by compliance domain, document type, and escalation level — then assigns them to the correct approval queue.
problem: At multi-regulator organisations like PHINMA and School for Life, governance documents arrive from board members, committee chairs, accreditation bodies, and government regulators — each requiring a different handling workflow. A policy change notice that should go to the full board lands in a committee inbox; an accreditation evidence submission sits unprocessed because no one realised it needed escalation. Manual classification is slow and inconsistent.
how_it_works: Applies a multi-label classifier to each incoming document, assigning type (policy change, accreditation evidence, financial report, complaint, audit finding), regulatory domain (government compliance, accreditation, internal governance, external funder), and urgency flag. Routes to the correct committee queue or compliance officer inbox. Logs routing decisions and tracks time-in-queue to surface governance bottlenecks.
ai_technique: Text classification with multi-label governance taxonomy
scoring_rationale: Functionally identical to idea 69 (Governance Classifier) in this same batch — both classify governance documents and route them to the appropriate body. As a standalone feature the problem is real but low-severity (misrouted documents cause delays, not crises) and easy to replicate without domain expertise.

name: Campus Operations Risk Triage
one_liner: Scores each school or campus site on composite operational risk — combining staffing gaps, maintenance backlogs, resource shortfalls, and delivery KPI deviations — so regional directors can allocate oversight effort to the sites that need it most.
problem: Networks operating 50-400 sites (Rising Academies across West Africa, PHINMA across the Philippines) cannot visit every campus regularly. Central teams rely on individual metric dashboards that show teacher turnover at one site, maintenance backlog at another, but never a combined picture. Sites with multiple compounding stressors — a staffing gap coinciding with a facility issue and declining attendance — get the same oversight attention as sites performing normally.
how_it_works: Ingests data from four operational domains per site: staffing (teacher vacancy rate, substitute usage days, rolling turnover), maintenance (unresolved ticket count, average age of open tickets), resource allocation (per-student spend variance from network median, supply delivery delays), and delivery KPIs (attendance rate, weekly learning outcome indicators where available). Calculates a composite risk score per site using weighted aggregation. Flags sites where multiple domains are in the red simultaneously. Outputs a weekly triage list ranked by composite risk, with a one-paragraph site risk narrative and recommended central intervention type (staffing support, maintenance escalation, resource reallocation).
ai_technique: Multi-factor risk scoring with site-level aggregation
scoring_rationale: Multi-site operators with 100+ campuses face a genuine daily resource allocation problem — central teams are flying blind without cross-domain risk synthesis. Named buyer (Regional Director) with clear operational authority, fast ROI once site risk rankings inform field visit schedules, and no credible existing tool in this niche.

name: Student Support Case Triager
one_liner: Classifies incoming student welfare and support requests by type and urgency — routing mental health flags, financial hardship cases, academic intervention requests, and discipline matters to the right team before anything falls through the cracks.
problem: Multi-site school networks and residential operators (CSPD, PHINMA, Kinetic) receive student support requests through email, intake forms, and informal counsellor notes. Volume is high enough that urgent welfare cases — a student showing safeguarding indicators, a financial hardship request with an imminent fee deadline — sit in a general inbox alongside routine information requests. Manual triage is error-prone under load, and a missed urgent flag carries serious duty-of-care risk.
how_it_works: Connects to student support intake channels (email, web form, counsellor note system). Applies a multi-class classifier to each submission, assigning support type (mental health and welfare, financial hardship, academic performance, behavioural issue) and urgency (immediate safety concern requiring same-day escalation, time-bound deadline, routine follow-up). Routes to the appropriate team queue — school counsellor, financial aid office, academic adviser, discipline committee. Automatically escalates submissions containing safeguarding-relevant language. Produces a daily triage summary for the Student Support Coordinator showing case volume, type distribution, and any unassigned urgent cases.
ai_technique: Multi-class text classification with priority scoring
buyer_persona: Student Support Coordinator / Dean of Students
scoring_rationale: Duty-of-care failures in student welfare are high-severity and headline-generating, making this a real pain with board-level consequences. Fast to build with current classification APIs, data lives in existing inboxes, and the buyer is clear. Lower defensibility as triage classification is not deeply novel — differentiation requires building the safeguarding vocabulary and escalation logic well.

name: Resource Requirements Estimator
one_liner: Predicts the teacher FTE, facility capacity, and material budget required for a new cohort or expansion site — using regression models fitted to the network's own historical cost and staffing data.
problem: When Rising Academies opens a new school or PHINMA launches a program at a new campus, resource planning relies on manual estimation by operations managers drawing on personal experience rather than data. This produces persistent errors: understaffed classrooms in the first term, facility overruns, excess materials purchased. Standard finance tools model generic business costs, not education-specific drivers like regulatory teacher-student ratio minimums, delivery mode differences (residential vs. commuter), or rural infrastructure premiums.
how_it_works: Builds regression models from two to four years of historical operational data across existing sites, linking enrollment size, program mix, delivery mode, and site characteristics (urban/rural, facility age, infrastructure type) to observed teacher FTE, facility utilisation, and material spend. When a new cohort or site is being planned, the estimator takes projected enrollment and site parameters as inputs and returns resource requirements with confidence ranges. Flags cost outliers in the plan relative to similar existing sites.
ai_technique: Regression estimation with education-specific cost drivers
scoring_rationale: CFO and Director of Operations are high-authority buyers with real planning pain, especially in fast-scaling networks. The model requires clean historical data across sites — feasible at mature networks like PHINMA, harder at smaller operators. Education-specific cost drivers provide moderate defensibility.

name: Operational Handover Summariser
one_liner: Reads shift handover notes, student welfare case updates, and maintenance logs and generates a concise structured summary — so the incoming coordinator can act on what matters in two minutes instead of twenty.
problem: In residential settings (Kinetic) and distributed school networks (CSPD, Rising Academies), the daily handover is a critical safety and operations checkpoint. Outgoing shift staff write freeform notes covering student welfare incidents, outstanding maintenance, medication records, and unresolved complaints. Incoming staff must read through long narrative logs under time pressure, and important items — a welfare concern buried in paragraph three, a maintenance hazard mentioned in passing — can be missed. There is no structured extraction layer between the raw notes and the person who must act on them.
how_it_works: Ingests shift notes, welfare case update files, and maintenance ticket exports at the end of each shift or school day. Applies a summarisation and entity extraction pipeline: pulls out named students of concern, outstanding actions with responsible owner, safety incidents, facility hazards, and items flagged for escalation. Generates a structured handover brief — top priorities, welfare flags, maintenance status, open actions — formatted for the incoming coordinator. Tags items containing safeguarding-relevant language for automatic escalation to the duty manager.
ai_technique: Text summarisation with education-specific entity tagging
scoring_rationale: Immediate time-to-value and easy data access (freeform text from existing note systems) make this quick to ship. The problem is genuine in residential settings where welfare oversight is a duty-of-care obligation. Weaker on business model — coordinator-level buyers have limited purchase authority — and low defensibility as document summarisation is now a commodity capability.
